title: Introduction to educabr
text: educabr provides, single canonical tidy schema, extensive set harmonised historical series Brazilian education available analytic format: Tertiary (higher-education) enrollment — 1907 2024, 118 years coverage, drawing seven distinct primary sources (IBGE Statistics 20th Century, Durham, Maduro Junior, Kang, INEP Synopsis, INEP Microdata, INEP CENSUP Power BI panel). Compulsory (fundamental) upper-secondary enrollment — 1933 2010, race/colour disaggregation 1960 onwards (Kang, Paese & Felix 2021). Mean years schooling adult population — 1925 2015, breakdowns sex, race/colour, macroregion state (Walter & Kang 2024). Every transformation auditable: output row carries canonical source key (catalogued inst/dict/vocabularies/sources.yaml) source_note exact table chapter origin.

title: Introduction to educabr
code: # From GitHub remotes::install_github("mancano-tales/educabr")

title: Introduction to educabr
text: public interface intentionally minimal: return long-format tibbles follow canonical schema (inst/dict/schema.yaml), columns geographic unit, inequality dimension (race, sex, …), source, value.
code: # Schemas of the two main return shapes str(get_enrollment(level = "fundamental", year = 1950)) #> tibble [2 × 16] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ year            : int [1:2] 1950 1950 #>  $ geo_level       : chr [1:2] "BR" "BR" #>  $ geo_code        : chr [1:2] "BR" "BR" #>  $ geo_name        : chr [1:2] "Brasil" "Brasil" #>  $ level           : chr [1:2] "fundamental" "fundamental" #>  $ network         : chr [1:2] "total" "total" #>  $ institution_type: chr [1:2] "total" "total" #>  $ modality        : chr [1:2] "total" "total" #>  $ dim_race        : chr [1:2] "total" "total" #>  $ age_group       : chr [1:2] "7-14" NA #>  $ indicator       : chr [1:2] "enrollment_rate" "enrollment_count" #>  $ value           : num [1:2] 4.55e+01 4.73e+06 #>  $ unit            : chr [1:2] "percent" "count" #>  $ source          : chr [1:2] "kang_paese_felix_2021" "kang_paese_felix_2021" #>  $ source_note     : chr [1:2] "Kang, Paese & Felix (2021). RHE 39(2):191-218. doi:10.1017/S0212610921000112" "Kang, Paese & Felix (2021). RHE 39(2):191-218. doi:10.1017/S0212610921000112" #>  $ is_derived      : logi [1:2] FALSE FALSE str(get_schooling(year = 1950)) #> tibble [1 × 12] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ year       : int 1950 #>  $ geo_level  : chr "BR" #>  $ geo_code   : chr "BR" #>  $ geo_name   : chr "Brasil" #>  $ dim_race   : chr "total" #>  $ dim_sex    : chr "total" #>  $ age_group  : chr NA #>  $ indicator  : chr "mean_years_schooling" #>  $ value      : num 1.59 #>  $ unit       : chr "years" #>  $ source     : chr "walter_kang_2023" #>  $ source_note: chr "Walter, J., & Kang, T. H. (2023). A new dataset of average years of schooling in Brazil, 1925-2015. FGV-IBRE working paper." #>  - attr(*, "educabr_meta")=List of 5 #>   ..$ build_script  : chr "data-raw/02_build_schooling_kang_fgv.R" #>   ..$ built_at      : POSIXct[1:1], format: "2026-05-17 19:08:24" #>   ..$ primary_source: chr "walter_kang_2023" #>   ..$ citation      : chr "Walter, J., & Kang, T. H. (2023). A new dataset of average years of schooling in Brazil, 1925-2015. FGV-IBRE working paper." #>   ..$ raw_files     : chr "data-raw/sources/kang_fgv_ibre_2023/3._anos_estudo_1925_2015_v_abril2023.xlsx"

what: Case 1 — gross enrollment rate by race/colour
title: Introduction to educabr
text: Using dimension = "race" can trace trajectory gross fundamental-education enrollment five IBGE race/colour categories 1960 2010: gap three main categories (white, black, brown) traces one central narratives sociological literature Brazilian education — relative convergence compulsory levels alongside persistent inequality higher levels.
code: fund_race <- get_enrollment(   level     = "fundamental",   indicator = "rate",   geo_level = "BR",   dimension = "race",   year      = c(1960, 2010) )  head(fund_race) #> # A tibble: 6 × 16 #>    year geo_level geo_code geo_name level      network institution_type modality #>   <int> <chr>     <chr>    <chr>    <chr>      <chr>   <chr>            <chr>    #> 1  1960 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> 2  1961 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> 3  1962 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> 4  1963 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> 5  1964 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> 6  1965 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> # ℹ 8 more variables: dim_race <chr>, age_group <chr>, indicator <chr>, #> #   value <dbl>, unit <chr>, source <chr>, source_note <chr>, is_derived <lgl>

what: Case 2 — multi-source comparison for tertiary enrollment
title: Introduction to educabr
text: Brazilian tertiary enrollment reconstructed multiple authors slightly different methodologies, especially long 1933–2000 period official sources sparse. educabr package keeps competing estimates side side, enabling direct comparison: 1980 several sources converge value (~1.38 million), suggests stabilised figure literature. years estimates diverge — passing source = "..." get_enrollment() recommended way pin single series secondary analysis.
code: ter_1980 <- get_enrollment(   level     = "superior",   year      = 1980,   network   = "total",   modality  = "total",   indicator = "count" )  ter_1980[, c("source", "value", "source_note")] #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   source                  value source_note                                      #>   <chr>                   <dbl> <chr>                                            #> 1 kang_paese_felix_2021 1377286 Kang, Paese & Felix (2021). RHE 39(2):191-218. … #> 2 durham_2005           1377286 Durham (2005). Educação superior, pública e pri… #> 3 kang_paese_felix_2021 1377286 Kang, Paese & Felix (2021), RHE 39(2):191-218. … #> 4 maduro_junior_2007    1377286 Maduro Junior (2007). Taxas de matrícula e gast…

title: Introduction to educabr
text: 2000 2008 INEP began collecting distance-learning (EAD) enrollment separate CENSUP table (tabela7.x), add total -person enrollment published tabela5.x. means headline “total” series interval published Kang, Durham, Maduro Junior, INEP’s synopsis systematically undercounted — ~700,000 enrollments 2008 reckoning. 2009 onwards CENSUP microdata already aggregate -person EAD registry, problem resolves . transition interval, educabr ships reconstructed totals combine -person component source INEP’s published EAD figures. rows carry is_derived = TRUE excluded default get_enrollment() output; pass include_derived = TRUE inspect : composite source key (e.g. kang_paese_felix_2021+inep_sinopse_censup) makes explicit combination: -person component comes first source EAD component comes second. source_note records exact composition citation purposes. Suggested citation convention: “Reconstructed total computed {-person source} (-person) INEP CENSUP Synopsis (EAD), following methodology documented educabr (Mançano 2026).”
code: recon <- get_enrollment(   level           = "superior",   year            = c(2000, 2008),   network         = "total",   modality        = "total",   indicator       = "count",   include_derived = TRUE )  recon[recon$is_derived, c("year", "source", "value")] #> # A tibble: 23 × 3 #>     year source                                      value #>    <int> <chr>                                       <dbl> #>  1  2000 durham_2005+inep_sinopse_censup           2695927 #>  2  2000 inep_sinopse_censup+inep_sinopse_censup   2695927 #>  3  2000 kang_paese_felix_2021+inep_sinopse_censup 2695927 #>  4  2000 maduro_junior_2007+inep_sinopse_censup    2695927 #>  5  2001 durham_2005+inep_sinopse_censup           3045113 #>  6  2001 inep_sinopse_censup+inep_sinopse_censup   3036113 #>  7  2001 inep_sinopse_censup+inep_sinopse_censup   3036113 #>  8  2001 kang_paese_felix_2021+inep_sinopse_censup 3036113 #>  9  2001 maduro_junior_2007+inep_sinopse_censup    3036113 #> 10  2003 inep_sinopse_censup+inep_sinopse_censup   3936933 #> # ℹ 13 more rows

what: Case 4 — mean years of schooling by sex
title: Introduction to educabr
text: series captures historical reversal gender gap: 1925 men averaged years schooling women; 20th century advantage inverts, 2015 women surpass men average attainment.
code: schooling_sex <- get_schooling(   geo_level = "BR",   dimension = "sex" )  tail(schooling_sex) #> # A tibble: 6 × 12 #>    year geo_level geo_code geo_name dim_race dim_sex age_group indicator   value #>   <int> <chr>     <chr>    <chr>    <chr>    <chr>   <chr>     <chr>       <dbl> #> 1  2010 BR        BR       Brasil   total    male    NA        mean_years…  8.03 #> 2  2011 BR        BR       Brasil   total    male    NA        mean_years…  8.2  #> 3  2012 BR        BR       Brasil   total    male    NA        mean_years…  8.39 #> 4  2013 BR        BR       Brasil   total    male    NA        mean_years…  8.49 #> 5  2014 BR        BR       Brasil   total    male    NA        mean_years…  8.63 #> 6  2015 BR        BR       Brasil   total    male    NA        mean_years…  8.72 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: unit <chr>, source <chr>, source_note <chr>

text: package ships Shiny dashboard three series: dashboard replicates multi-source tertiary comparison interactively. tab “View R code” button emits educabr + ggplot2 snippet needed reproduce visualisation local R session — direct bridge dashboard exploration reproducible scripted analysis.

title: Introduction to educabr
text: Every series carries canonical source key (source) full reference catalogued inst/dict/vocabularies/sources.yaml. seven primary tertiary sources : Kang, T. H., Paese, L. H. Z., & Felix, N. F. . (2021). Late unequal. Revista de Historia Económica 39(2), 191-218. doi:10.1017/S0212610921000112 Kang, T. H., & Menetrier, . (2024). Políticas elitistas e despesas públicas em educação. Estudos Econômicos 54(3). doi:10.1590/1980-53575434tkim Kang, T. H., Menetrier, ., & Comim, F. (2024). side effects big push growth strategy. RHE 42(3), 387-414. doi:10.1017/S0212610924000120 Walter, J. R., & Kang, T. H. (2024). new dataset average years schooling Brazil. Economic History Developing Regions 39(3), 307-336. doi:10.1080/20780389.2024.2417268 Durham, E. R. (2005). Educação superior, pública e privada. Schwartzman (ed.), Os desafios da educação Brasil, pp. 191-233. Maduro Junior, P. R. R. M. (2007). Taxas de matrícula e gastos em educação Brasil [MSc dissertation, FGV/EPGE]. hdl:10438/110 IBGE (2007). Estatísticas Século XX. seculoxx.ibge.gov.br INEP/MEC. Sinopse Estatística da Educação Superior (1995-2008). INEP/MEC. Microdados Censo da Educação Superior (2009-2024). INEP/MEC. CENSUP Power BI panel (2010-2024). cite package : Mançano, T. (2026). educabr: Harmonized Historical Series Brazilian Education (version 0.0.0.9000). https://github.com/mancano-tales/educabr

title: Introdução ao educabr (PT-BR)
text: O educabr disponibiliza, sob um único esquema tidy canônico, o conjunto mais extenso de séries históricas harmonizadas sobre educação brasileira disponível em formato analítico: Matrículas ensino superior — 1907 2024, 118 anos de cobertura, articulando sete fontes primárias distintas (IBGE Estatísticas Século XX, Durham, Maduro Junior, Kang, INEP Sinopse, INEP Microdados e o painel CENSUP INEP). Matrículas ensino fundamental e médio — 1933 2010, com desagregação por cor/raça partir de 1960 (Kang, Paese & Felix 2021). Anos médios de escolaridade da população adulta — 1925 2015, com desagregação por sexo, cor/raça, macrorregião e unidade da federação (Walter & Kang 2024). Toda transformação dos dados é auditável: cada linha output carrega o source (chave canônica em inst/dict/vocabularies/sources.yaml) e um source_note com referência exata da tabela ou capítulo de origem.

title: Introdução ao educabr (PT-BR)
code: # via GitHub remotes::install_github("mancano-tales/educabr")

title: Introdução ao educabr (PT-BR)
text: Toda interface pública gira em torno de duas funções: Ambas retornam tibbles longas seguindo o mesmo esquema canônico (inst/dict/schema.yaml), com colunas para unidade geográfica, dimensão de desigualdade (raça, sexo, etc.), fonte e o valor.
code: # Esquema dos dois principais resultados str(get_enrollment(level = "fundamental", year = 1950)) #> tibble [2 × 16] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ year            : int [1:2] 1950 1950 #>  $ geo_level       : chr [1:2] "BR" "BR" #>  $ geo_code        : chr [1:2] "BR" "BR" #>  $ geo_name        : chr [1:2] "Brasil" "Brasil" #>  $ level           : chr [1:2] "fundamental" "fundamental" #>  $ network         : chr [1:2] "total" "total" #>  $ institution_type: chr [1:2] "total" "total" #>  $ modality        : chr [1:2] "total" "total" #>  $ dim_race        : chr [1:2] "total" "total" #>  $ age_group       : chr [1:2] "7-14" NA #>  $ indicator       : chr [1:2] "enrollment_rate" "enrollment_count" #>  $ value           : num [1:2] 4.55e+01 4.73e+06 #>  $ unit            : chr [1:2] "percent" "count" #>  $ source          : chr [1:2] "kang_paese_felix_2021" "kang_paese_felix_2021" #>  $ source_note     : chr [1:2] "Kang, Paese & Felix (2021). RHE 39(2):191-218. doi:10.1017/S0212610921000112" "Kang, Paese & Felix (2021). RHE 39(2):191-218. doi:10.1017/S0212610921000112" #>  $ is_derived      : logi [1:2] FALSE FALSE str(get_schooling(year = 1950)) #> tibble [1 × 12] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ year       : int 1950 #>  $ geo_level  : chr "BR" #>  $ geo_code   : chr "BR" #>  $ geo_name   : chr "Brasil" #>  $ dim_race   : chr "total" #>  $ dim_sex    : chr "total" #>  $ age_group  : chr NA #>  $ indicator  : chr "mean_years_schooling" #>  $ value      : num 1.59 #>  $ unit       : chr "years" #>  $ source     : chr "walter_kang_2023" #>  $ source_note: chr "Walter, J., & Kang, T. H. (2023). A new dataset of average years of schooling in Brazil, 1925-2015. what: Caso 1 — taxa bruta de matrícula por raça/cor
title: Introdução ao educabr (PT-BR)
text: Aqui usamos desagregação dimension = "race" para comparar trajetória de matrícula ensino fundamental entre os cinco grupos de cor/raça IBGE entre 1960 e 2010. diferença entre três principais categorias (white, black, brown) acompanha uma das narrativas centrais da literatura sociológica sobre educação Brasil — convergência relativa nas matrículas obrigatórias acompanhada de persistente desigualdade em níveis mais altos.
code: ef_raca <- get_enrollment(   level     = "fundamental",   indicator = "rate",   geo_level = "BR",   dimension = "race",   year      = c(1960, 2010) )  head(ef_raca) #> # A tibble: 6 × 16 #>    year geo_level geo_code geo_name level      network institution_type modality #>   <int> <chr>     <chr>    <chr>    <chr>      <chr>   <chr>            <chr>    #> 1  1960 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> 2  1961 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> 3  1962 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> 4  1963 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> 5  1964 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> 6  1965 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> # ℹ 8 more variables: dim_race <chr>, age_group <chr>, indicator <chr>, #> #   value <dbl>, unit <chr>, source <chr>, source_note <chr>, is_derived <lgl>

what: Caso 2 — comparação multi-fonte no ensino superior
title: Introdução ao educabr (PT-BR)
text: O ensino superior brasileiro foi reconstituído por diversos autores com metodologias ligeiramente diferentes, sobretudo para o longo período 1933-2000 em que fontes oficiais são esparsas. O educabr mantém todas estimativas concorrentes lado lado, permitindo comparação direta: Note que para 1980 várias fontes convergem para o mesmo valor (~1,38 milhão), o que sugere uma estimativa estabilizada na literatura. Para outros anos estimativas divergem — passar source = "..." get_enrollment() é o caminho recomendado para fixar uma série única em análises secundárias.
code: es_1980 <- get_enrollment(   level     = "superior",   year      = 1980,   network   = "total",   modality  = "total",   indicator = "count" )  es_1980[, c("source", "value", "source_note")] #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   source                  value source_note                                      #>   <chr>                   <dbl> <chr>                                            #> 1 kang_paese_felix_2021 1377286 Kang, Paese & Felix (2021). RHE 39(2):191-218. … #> 2 durham_2005           1377286 Durham (2005). Educação superior, pública e pri… #> 3 kang_paese_felix_2021 1377286 Kang, Paese & Felix (2021), RHE 39(2):191-218. … #> 4 maduro_junior_2007    1377286 Maduro Junior (2007). Taxas de matrícula e gast…

what: Caso 3 — o problema dos totais reconstruídos (2000-2008)
title: Introdução ao educabr (PT-BR)
text: Entre 2000 e 2008 o INEP começou coletar matrículas em EAD (educação distância) em uma tabela específica CENSUP (tabela7.x), porém não somou ao total das matrículas presenciais publicadas na tabela5.x. Isso significa que séries “totais” desse intervalo publicadas por Kang, Durham, Maduro Junior e pela própria sinopse INEP estão sistematicamente subestimadas — em até 700 mil matrículas em 2008, segundo nosso cálculo. partir de 2009, os microdados CENSUP já agregam presencial e EAD mesmo cadastro, e o problema se resolve. Para esse intervalo de transição, o educabr disponibiliza totais reconstruídos (reconstructed enrollment totals) que somam o componente presencial de cada fonte ao EAD publicado pelo INEP. Essas linhas têm is_derived = TRUE e ficam fora resultado padrão de get_enrollment(); para inspecioná-las basta passar include_derived = TRUE: O nome composto source (ex.: kang_paese_felix_2021+inep_sinopse_censup) explicita que se trata de uma combinação: o componente presencial vem da primeira fonte e o componente EAD vem da segunda. O source_note registra composição exata para fins de citação. Convenção de citação sugerida: “Total reconstruído computado partir de {fonte_presencial} (presencial) e INEP Sinopse CENSUP (EAD), conforme metodologia documentada em educabr (Mançano 2026).”
code: recon <- get_enrollment(   level           = "superior",   year            = c(2000, 2008),   network         = "total",   modality        = "total",   indicator       = "count",   include_derived = TRUE )  recon[recon$is_derived, c("year", "source", "value")] #> # A tibble: 23 × 3 #>     year source                                      value #>    <int> <chr>                                       <dbl> #>  1  2000 durham_2005+inep_sinopse_censup           2695927 #>  2  2000 inep_sinopse_censup+inep_sinopse_censup   2695927 #>  3  2000 kang_paese_felix_2021+inep_sinopse_censup 2695927 #>  4  2000 maduro_junior_2007+inep_sinopse_censup    2695927 #>  5  2001 durham_2005+inep_sinopse_censup           3045113 #>  6  2001 inep_sinopse_censup+inep_sinopse_censup   3036113 #>  7  2001 inep_sinopse_censup+inep_sinopse_censup   3036113 #>  8  2001 kang_paese_felix_2021+inep_sinopse_censup 3036113 #>  9  2001 maduro_junior_2007+inep_sinopse_censup    3036113 #> 10  2003 inep_sinopse_censup+inep_sinopse_censup   3936933 #> # ℹ 13 more rows

what: Caso 4 — anos médios de escolaridade por sexo
title: Introdução ao educabr (PT-BR)
text: série mostra inversão histórica gender gap: em 1925 os homens tinham em média mais anos de estudo que mulheres; ao longo século XX vantagem inverte e em 2015 mulheres ultrapassam homens em escolaridade média.
code: escolaridade_sexo <- get_schooling(   geo_level = "BR",   dimension = "sex" )  tail(escolaridade_sexo) #> # A tibble: 6 × 12 #>    year geo_level geo_code geo_name dim_race dim_sex age_group indicator   value #>   <int> <chr>     <chr>    <chr>    <chr>    <chr>   <chr>     <chr>       <dbl> #> 1  2010 BR        BR       Brasil   total    male    NA        mean_years…  8.03 #> 2  2011 BR        BR       Brasil   total    male    NA        mean_years…  8.2  #> 3  2012 BR        BR       Brasil   total    male    NA        mean_years…  8.39 #> 4  2013 BR        BR       Brasil   total    male    NA        mean_years…  8.49 #> 5  2014 BR        BR       Brasil   total    male    NA        mean_years…  8.63 #> 6  2015 BR        BR       Brasil   total    male    NA        mean_years…  8.72 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: unit <chr>, source <chr>, source_note <chr>

title: Introdução ao educabr (PT-BR)
text: O pacote acompanha um painel Shiny com três séries: O painel reproduz toda comparação multi-fonte ensino superior de forma interativa, com um botão “View R code” em cada aba que gera o código educabr + ggplot2 para reproduzir visualização seu ambiente R local — uma ponte direta entre exploração painel e análise reprodutível em script.
code: educabr::run_dashboard()

title: Introdução ao educabr (PT-BR)
text: lista completa de fontes com DOIs e links está na vignette principal em inglês (vignette("01-introduction", "educabr")). Para citar o pacote em si: Mançano, T. (2026). educabr: Harmonized Historical Series Brazilian Education (versão 0.0.0.9000). https://github.com/mancano-tales/educabr

title: educabr
text: Harmonized historical series Brazilian education — enrollment educational attainment, compiled reconciled across decades heterogeneous official academic sources.  🇧🇷 PT-BR: educabr reúne séries históricas tratadas sobre educação formal Brasil — matrículas por nível e rede, anos de estudo, alfabetização e atingimento educacional — compiladas de fontes oficiais e acadêmicas heterogêneas (Censo Escolar, PNAD, Censo Demográfico, Anuário Estatístico IBGE, Paglayan 2022, entre outras) e reconciliadas em um único schema tidy, com proveniência explícita. 🇺🇸 EN: educabr provides curated long-run series Brazilian formal education — enrollment stage network, years schooling, literacy attainment — compiled reconciled heterogeneous official academic sources single tidy schema explicit provenance.

title: educabr
text: 🚧 Em desenvolvimento ativo (v0.0.x). O pacote está na fase M1 roadmap: schema canônico, pipeline ETL e esqueleto definidos; sem funções de usuário ainda. Veja seção Roadmap.

title: educabr
text: educabr é complementar ao educabR de Sidney Bissoli, que organiza acesso por fonte oficial (get_ideb(), get_enem(), get_censo_escolar(), …). Aqui o eixo é por tema e série histórica: indicadores únicos compilados de múltiplas fontes ao longo tempo. Convenções de nomenclatura, schema de saída e utilidades de cache são compatíveis, com vistas uma eventual fusão. Inspirações de design: geobr (família de funções coerente), PNADCperiods (dashboard embutido + entrega metodológica) e o ecossistema brverse.

title: educabr
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("mancano-tales/educabr")

previous_headings: Como vai funcionar (preview da API v0.4)
title: educabr
text: Quem está estudando em determinada etapa e ano — fluxo anual, perspectiva da escola.
code: library(educabr)  # Taxa líquida de matrícula no fundamental, por raça, 1980-2020 get_enrollment(   level     = "fundamental",   indicator = "net_rate",   dimension = "race",   year      = 1980:2020 )

previous_headings: Como vai funcionar (preview da API v0.4)
title: educabr
text: Quanto população já estudou — estoque acumulado, perspectiva da pessoa.
code: # Média de anos de estudo da população 25+, por raça get_schooling(   measure   = "years_of_schooling",   dimension = "race",   age_group = "25+",   year      = 1980:2020 )

previous_headings: Como vai funcionar (preview da API v0.4)
what: Desigualdade como recorte, não como dataset
title: educabr
text: Desigualdade educacional não é um dado pronto: é um recorte aplicado um indicador. Toda função get_*() aceita o argumento dimension (race, sex, income, location). O helper compute_gap() calcula hiatos partir output dessas funções:
code: get_schooling(measure = "years_of_schooling", dimension = "race",               year = 1980:2020) |>   compute_gap(by = "dim_race", ref = "white", method = "difference")

previous_headings: Como vai funcionar (preview da API v0.4)
title: educabr
text: Também publicado em shinyapps.io (link após M5).
code: educabr::run_dashboard()       # Shiny app local

title: educabr
text: Todos os get_*() devolvem um tibble schema tidy documentado em inst/dict/schema.yaml: uma linha por observação, fontes alternativas para o mesmo indicador como linhas distintas (coluna source), agregações como nível de fator explícito ("total"), nunca como NA. Vocabulários controlados de indicadores e fontes ficam em inst/dict/vocabularies/.

title: educabr
text: Após o primeiro release com DOI Zenodo:
code: citation("educabr") educabr_cite("paglayan_2022")   # cita também a fonte original do indicador

title: educabr
text: Issues e PRs são bem-vindos. Para contribuir com dados, abra uma issue descrevendo planilha-fonte, autor, cobertura geográfica/temporal e licença — antes de mandar PR. Para regenerar o hex sticker pacote: O script lê man/figures/raw/brasil.png (silhueta geobr) e produz man/figures/logo.png via hexSticker.
code: source("man/figures/logo.R")

title: educabr
text: Código sob GPL (>= 3). Dados sob CC 4.0 (exceto onde fonte original impuser restrições; consulte inst/dict/vocabularies/sources.yaml). Veja LICENSE.md.

what: educabr: Harmonized Historical Series on Brazilian Education — educabr-package
title: educabr: Harmonized Historical Series on Brazilian Education — educabr-package
text: Curated long-run series Brazilian formal education — enrollment educational attainment — compiled heterogeneous official academic sources single tidy schema explicit provenance.

title: educabr: Harmonized Historical Series on Brazilian Education — educabr-package
text: canonical output schema (one row per observation; sources separate rows) documented inst/dict/schema.yaml. Inequality dataset cut applied indicators: every get_*() function accepts dimension argument returns indicator broken race, sex, income location. helper compute_gap() (planned) turns long-format breakdowns usual gap/ratio metrics.

title: educabr: Harmonized Historical Series on Brazilian Education — educabr-package
text: v0 lays data schema, ETL pipeline package skeleton. first user-facing functions — get_enrollment() get_schooling() — bundled Shiny dashboard (run_dashboard()) scheduled v0.2–v0.4 milestones. See NEWS.md released.

what: Enrollment series — Kang / FGV-IBRE 2023 compilation — enrollment_kang_fgv
title: Enrollment series — Kang / FGV-IBRE 2023 compilation — enrollment_kang_fgv
text: Long-run Brazilian school enrollment data compiled harmonized Kang, Paese & Felix (2021) refreshed FGV/IBRE April 2023. dataset internal backing store consumed get_enrollment(); end-users call function rather loading object directly, applies filters, label translation, optional pivoting.

title: Enrollment series — Kang / FGV-IBRE 2023 compilation — enrollment_kang_fgv
code: enrollment_kang_fgv

title: Enrollment series — Kang / FGV-IBRE 2023 compilation — enrollment_kang_fgv
text: tibble 6 238 rows 13 columns: year integer. Reference year observation (1871–2010). geo_level character. Geographic aggregation level: "BR" (national) "UF" (state). geo_code character. Geographic code: "BR" national; IBGE 2-letter UF abbreviation (e.g. "SP", "BA") states. geo_name character. Human-readable geographic name Portuguese (e.g. "Brasil", "São Paulo"). level character. Education stage using educabr vocabulary: "fundamental_anos_iniciais", "fundamental_anos_finais", "fundamental", "medio", "superior". network character. Administrative dependency. Always "total" source (network breakdown available). dim_race character. Race/colour dimension using educabr vocabulary ("white", "black", "brown", "asian", "indigenous"). "total" disaggregated. Race breakdown available BR level 1960–2010 . age_group character. Reference age bracket rate indicator (e.g. "7-14", "15-17", "18-24"). NA count indicators, headcounts tied population bracket. indicator character. Indicator key: "enrollment_count" (absolute headcount) "enrollment_rate" (gross enrollment rate, 0–100 scale). value double. Numeric value indicator. unit character. Unit measurement: "count" "percent". source character. Compact source key: "kang_fgv_ibre_2023". Full metadata inst/dict/vocabularies/sources.yaml. source_note character. Inline bibliographic reference.

title: Enrollment series — Kang / FGV-IBRE 2023 compilation — enrollment_kang_fgv
text: Kang, T., Paese, ., & Felix, R. (2021). Late Unequal: Historical Enrollment Rates Brazil, 1871–2010. Revista de Historia Económica, 39(2), 191–218. doi:10.1017/S0212610920000099 . Data compilation: FGV/IBRE (April 2023 revision). ETL script: data-raw/01_build_enrollment_kang_fgv.R.

title: Enrollment series — Kang / FGV-IBRE 2023 compilation — enrollment_kang_fgv
text: Column definitions, primary-key constraints, unit conventions specified inst/dict/schema.yaml. dataset validated schema every time rebuilt (see data-raw/01_build_enrollment_kang_fgv.R).

what: Tertiary (ensino superior) enrollment — multi-source compilation — enrollment_tertiary
title: Tertiary (ensino superior) enrollment — multi-source compilation — enrollment_tertiary
text: National-level (BR) Brazilian higher-education enrollment 1907 2024, harmonised across seven different primary sources. dataset consumed get_enrollment() level = "superior". Multiple sources (year, network) kept purpose users can compare competing estimates — pass source = "..." lock specific series.

title: Tertiary (ensino superior) enrollment — multi-source compilation — enrollment_tertiary
code: enrollment_tertiary

title: Tertiary (ensino superior) enrollment — multi-source compilation — enrollment_tertiary
text: tibble approximately 1 350 rows 16 columns matching canonical schema (inst/dict/schema.yaml). tertiary-specific columns : level Always "superior". network Administrative dependency: federal, estadual, municipal, publica, privada, plus private sub-categories (privada_particular / privada_comunitaria_confessional_filantropica pre-2009, privada_lucrativa / privada_nao_lucrativa post-2009), especial, total. institution_type INEP/MEC institutional category: university, university_center, faculty, faculty_school_institute, integrated_faculty, integrated_faculty_university_center, technology_center, technology_center_fat, cefet_ifet, isolated_establishment, total. modality presencial, ead, total (available 2000 onwards). is_derived TRUE row reconstructed total — computed total combining -person enrollment one source EAD enrollment INEP, used fix 2000-2008 transition gap original sources excluded EAD nominal totals. Filtered get_enrollment() unless include_derived = TRUE.

title: Tertiary (ensino superior) enrollment — multi-source compilation — enrollment_tertiary
text: Compilation curated package author (Mançano, 2026) publicly available sources. Raw file: data-raw/sources/tertiary_multisource/data_tertiary_v6_clean.xlsx. ETL: data-raw/03_build_enrollment_tertiary.R.

title: Tertiary (ensino superior) enrollment — multi-source compilation — enrollment_tertiary
text: ibge_seculo_xx — Anuários Estatísticos 1908-1980. durham_2005 — Durham (2005). maduro_junior_2007 — Maduro Junior MSc dissertation. kang_paese_felix_2021 — Kang, Paese & Felix RHE paper. inep_sinopse_censup — INEP Sinopse 1995-2008. inep_microdados_censup — INEP microdata 2009-2024. inep_censup_powerbi — INEP Power BI panel. derived rows source column concatenation "<presencial_key>+<ead_key>"; exact composition documented source_note.

title: Brazilian school-enrollment series — get_enrollment
text: Returns harmonized school-enrollment counts rates, optionally broken color/race, administrative network, institutional type, teaching modality. Series pulled enrollment datasets shipped package concatenated canonical long-format schema.

title: Brazilian school-enrollment series — get_enrollment
code: get_enrollment(   level = NULL,   network = NULL,   institution_type = NULL,   modality = NULL,   year = NULL,   geo_level = c("BR", "UF"),   geo = NULL,   dimension = c("none", "race"),   indicator = NULL,   source = NULL,   include_derived = FALSE,   wide = FALSE,   lang = c("en", "pt") )

title: Brazilian school-enrollment series — get_enrollment
text: level Character vector one stage codes: "fundamental_anos_iniciais", "fundamental_anos_finais", "fundamental", "medio", "superior". NULL (default) means filter. network Character vector administrative-network codes ("federal", "estadual", "municipal", "publica", "privada", plus post-2009 private subcategories "privada_particular", "privada_comunitaria_confessional_filantropica", "privada_lucrativa", "privada_nao_lucrativa", "especial", "total"). NULL (default) means filter. institution_type Character vector restricting institutional category (meaningful level = "superior"). See inst/dict/schema.yaml controlled vocabulary. NULL (default) means filter; pass "total" keep rows aggregate across institutional types. modality Character vector: "presencial", "ead", "total". NULL (default) means filter. year Integer vector two-element c(min, max) range. NULL years. geo_level One "BR" (national, default) "UF" (state). geo Character vector 2-letter UF codes geo_level = "UF". NULL (default) returns UFs. dimension Inequality breakdown. One "none" (default, totals ) "race". Future versions add "sex", "income", "location". indicator Character vector. "count" counts, "rate" gross enrollment rates. NULL returns . source Character vector source keys (see inst/dict/vocabularies/sources.yaml). NULL returns available sources. Tip: (year, level, network) covered multiple sources (common tertiary panel), pass source = "..." lock single series. include_derived Logical. FALSE (default), excludes -called reconstructed totals — rows value computed combining components different sources (typically -person enrollment single-source paper plus EAD enrollment INEP, 2000-2008 original sources -reported combined total). Set TRUE include . composition documented source_note; source column rows carries composite key "<presencial_source>+<ead_source>". effect datasets carry is_derived flag. wide Logical. TRUE, pivots indicator column wide form (one column per indicator). Default FALSE. lang One "en" (default) "pt". "pt", factor levels translated using inst/dict/i18n.yaml.

title: Brazilian school-enrollment series — get_enrollment
text: tibble following canonical schema inst/dict/schema.yaml. Optional columns (institution_type, modality, is_derived) present whenever loaded datasets carries ; rows coming datasets without column value defaults "total" (FALSE is_derived).

title: Brazilian school-enrollment series — get_enrollment
code: if (FALSE) { # National series, ensino fundamental, all years get_enrollment(level = "fundamental", geo_level = "BR")  # Tertiary enrollment, all sources, compare them get_enrollment(level = "superior", network = "total", modality = "total")  # Tertiary private particular only, post-2000 get_enrollment(level = "superior", network = "privada_particular",                year = c(2000, 2024))  # Compare with derived rows included get_enrollment(level = "superior", network = "total",                year = c(2000, 2008), include_derived = TRUE) }

what: Brazilian mean years of schooling — get_schooling
title: Brazilian mean years of schooling — get_schooling
text: Returns harmonized series average years schooling national, macro-region, state level, optionally broken color/race sex. bundled data comes Walter & Kang (2023), FGV-IBRE working paper reconstructs series 1925 2015.

title: Brazilian mean years of schooling — get_schooling
code: get_schooling(   year = NULL,   geo_level = c("BR", "region", "UF"),   geo = NULL,   dimension = c("none", "race", "sex"),   source = NULL,   wide = FALSE,   lang = c("en", "pt") )

title: Brazilian mean years of schooling — get_schooling
text: year Integer vector two-element c(min, max) range. NULL years. geo_level One "BR" (national, default), "region" (macro-region), "UF" (state). Region UF series start 1950. geo Character vector geographic codes. geo_level = "UF", 2-letter IBGE UF abbreviations (e.g. "SP", "BA"). geo_level = "region", one "N", "NE", "CO", "SE", "S". NULL (default) returns geographies level. dimension Inequality breakdown. One : "none" (default) — national totals (race sex split); "race" — breakdown IBGE color/race (white, black, brown, asian, indigenous), totals across sex; "sex" — breakdown sex (male, female), totals across race. Race sub-national breakdowns available geo_level =   "BR". source Character vector source keys. NULL returns available sources (currently "walter_kang_2023"). wide Logical. TRUE, pivots result wide form. indicator effect minimal (one indicator column), parameter provided API consistency get_enrollment(). Default FALSE. lang One "en" (default) "pt". "pt", factor levels translated via inst/dict/i18n.yaml.

title: Brazilian mean years of schooling — get_schooling
text: tibble following canonical schema inst/dict/schema.yaml. Columns: year, geo_level, geo_code, geo_name, dim_race, dim_sex, age_group, indicator, value, unit, source, source_note. level network columns omitted (applicable population-level attainment averages).

title: Brazilian mean years of schooling — get_schooling
code: if (FALSE) { # National series, all years get_schooling()  # By race, 1960-2015 get_schooling(dimension = "race", year = c(1960, 2015))  # By sex across states get_schooling(dimension = "sex", geo_level = "UF", geo = c("SP", "BA")) }

text: lang Default UI language. One "pt" (default) "en". ... Passed shiny::runApp() (e.g. port, launch.browser, host).

title: Launch the educabr Shiny dashboard — run_dashboard
text: Invoked side effect (starts Shiny app). Returns value shiny::runApp() invisibly.

title: Launch the educabr Shiny dashboard — run_dashboard
text: dashboard also deployed publicly (link README) users prefer install R locally.

what: Mean years of schooling — Walter & Kang 2023 compilation — schooling_kang_fgv
title: Mean years of schooling — Walter & Kang 2023 compilation — schooling_kang_fgv
text: Long-run Brazilian mean years schooling reconstructed Walter & Kang (2023) Censuses household surveys. dataset internal backing store consumed get_schooling(); end-users call function rather loading object directly.

title: Mean years of schooling — Walter & Kang 2023 compilation — schooling_kang_fgv
code: schooling_kang_fgv

title: Mean years of schooling — Walter & Kang 2023 compilation — schooling_kang_fgv
text: tibble approximately 2 300 rows 12 columns: year integer. Reference year (1925–2015). BR-level series starts 1925; region UF series start 1950. geo_level character. "BR", "region", "UF". geo_code character. "BR" national; IBGE single/double- letter macro-region code ("N", "NE", "CO", "SE", "S"); IBGE 2-letter UF code. geo_name character. Human-readable geographic name Portuguese. dim_race character. Race/colour dimension: "white", "black", "brown", "asian", "indigenous", "total". Race breakdown available BR level . dim_sex character. Sex: "male", "female", "total". Sex breakdown available BR level . age_group character. Always NA dataset; included schema compatibility. indicator character. Always "mean_years_schooling". value double. Mean years schooling adult population. unit character. Always "years". source character. Always "walter_kang_2023". source_note character. Inline bibliographic reference.

title: Mean years of schooling — Walter & Kang 2023 compilation — schooling_kang_fgv
text: Walter, J., & Kang, T. H. (2023). new dataset average years schooling Brazil, 1925-2015. FGV-IBRE working paper. ETL script: data-raw/02_build_schooling_kang_fgv.R.

title: educabr (development version)
text: enrollment_kang_fgv — 6,238 rows. Brazilian school enrollment counts gross rates stage (EF1, EF2, EF, EM, ES), 1933-2010 national level 1955-2010 UF level, breakdown colour/race 1960-2010. Per-paper source attribution (kang_paese_felix_2021, kang_menetrier_2024, kang_menetrier_comim_2024). enrollment_tertiary — 1,341 rows. Brazilian tertiary enrollment 1907-2024 compiled across seven primary sources: IBGE Estatísticas Século XX, Durham (2005), Maduro Junior (2007), Kang/Paese/Felix (2021), INEP CENSUP Synopsis (1995-2008), INEP CENSUP Microdata (2009-2024), INEP CENSUP Power BI panel. Multiple sources per year-network kept purpose support cross-source comparison. Includes 25 reconstructed total rows (is_derived = TRUE) fill 2000-2008 transition period INEP published -person EAD enrollment separately. schooling_kang_fgv — 2,287 rows. Mean years schooling adult population, 1925-2015 (BR), 1950-2015 (region, UF), sex race breakdowns BR level (Walter & Kang 2024).

title: educabr (development version)
text: get_enrollment() — long-format access enrollment series filters level, network, institution_type, modality, year, geo_level/geo, dimension, indicator, source, include_derived. Returns canonical schema English labels (lang = "en") PT-BR labels (lang = "pt"). get_schooling() — long-format access mean-years--schooling series filters year, geo_level/geo, dimension, source, lang. run_dashboard() — launches bundled Shiny dashboard locally.

title: educabr (development version)
text: Three-theme navbar (English UI): Enrollment, Tertiary Education, Educational Attainment. Tertiary Education tab features multi-source comparison interaction-based colour palette (source × modality combination gets unique colour shade), shape--source, linetype--modality encoding. “View R code” button every tab generates self-contained, copy-pasteable R snippet (educabr + ggplot2) reproduces current chart locally.

title: educabr (development version)
text: Canonical tidy-long schema documented inst/dict/schema.yaml primary-key constraints, year domain, controlled vocabularies factor levels, conventions missing values. 13 primary source entries documented inst/dict/vocabularies/sources.yaml DOIs, URLs, coverage metadata. PT-BR labels every factor level inst/dict/i18n.yaml.

title: educabr (development version)
text: data-raw/01_build_enrollment_kang_fgv.R — Kang/FGV-IBRE 2023 compilation (4 xlsx files → enrollment_kang_fgv.rda). data-raw/02_build_schooling_kang_fgv.R — Walter & Kang 2024 series (1 xlsx file → schooling_kang_fgv.rda). data-raw/03_build_enrollment_tertiary.R — multi-source tertiary compilation, canonicalisation 69 raw source strings 7 canonical keys 4 composite derived-row keys, plus exact-duplicate deduplication.

title: educabr (development version)
text: 9 tests get_enrollment() (core filters pivots). 9 tests get_schooling(). 7 tests tertiary-specific arguments (institution_type, modality, include_derived, composite source keys, loader normalisation legacy datasets).
